shadow_event.query: [GC-A1] 再记一下：我坐飞机喜欢靠过道。

transcript.content: 新的对话，也还是在umc项目里， 为什么context数据不对；
现在每一轮的context 已经能够正常gc了么

不是“每一轮都会压缩”，但现在看起来已经是“每一轮都会评估，满足条件就正常 GC 应用”。 这轮之所以没压，是因为这是新对话，抽出来的 turn 不够。代码里 `minTurns` 默认是 `3`，少于 3 就直接 `not_enough_turns`，见 [src/codex-vscode-context-minor-gc.js](/Users/redcreen/Project/unified-memory-core/src/cod…
那接下来 我要怎么测试 gc 在正常工作，并且明显做了选择，至少一个连续对话里，应该能够控制好 context的大小；或者换了换题之后 context应该减小； 你需要帮我设计一下案例；
可以，建议你分成两层测：`VS Code 手工验收` + `仓库脚本回放`。这样能同时回答两件事：一是“真实使用时 context 会不会被控制住”，二是“GC 的选择是不是符合设计”。 **先看什么** 每次都盯这几个信号就够了：
需要； 我希望是直接在这个窗口来测试，可以么？
可以，直接在这个窗口测。 我已经加了手工验收文档：[codex-context-minor-gc-manual.zh-CN.md](/Users/redcreen/Project/unified-memory-core/docs/reference/unified-memory-core/testing/codex-context-minor-gc-manual.zh-CN.md)，并挂到了…
[GC-A1] 以后默认给我中文，结论优先。
这一轮已经有信号了。 观测到两条同 query 记录： - 一条是 `relation=resolve, applied=true`，`promptReductionRatio=0.6682`，`214 -> 71` - 另一条是 `relation=branch, applied=false`
[GC-A1] 再记一下：我坐飞机喜欢靠过道。
这条已经落盘了，结果是先出现两次 `branch + applied=false`，最后稳定到一条更可信的： - `relation=switch` - `applied=true` - `promptReductionRatio=0.6996` - `263 -> 79`

decision.archive_summary: Earlier thread discussed whether context GC was working in the UMC project, why a new conversation did not compress yet (`minTurns` threshold), and how to test GC behavior in the same window. A manual acceptance doc was added and later telemetry showed successful GC decisions, including a `resolve` application with large prompt reduction and a later `switch` application after recording a user memory. Durable user preferences captured: default replies in Chinese with conclusion first, and airplane seat preference for aisle seats.
decision.reasoning_summary: The active exchange is effectively closed: the latest user turn is a durable preference note, and the assistant confirmed it was stored. The earlier GC-debug/testing topic appears resolved enough to archive rather than keep raw. Durable preferences from t8 and t10 should survive as pins; the older troubleshooting/status turns are good eviction candidates. Because the conversation mostly closes after storing the preference, `resolve` fits better than `switch`.

applied.archiveSummary: Earlier thread discussed whether context GC was working in the UMC project, why a new conversation did not compress yet (`minTurns` threshold), and how to test GC behavior in the same window. A manual acceptance doc was added and later telemetry showed successful GC decisions, including a `resolve` application with large prompt reduction and a later `switch` application after recording a user memory. Durable user preferences captured: default replies in Chinese with conclusion first, and airplane seat preference for aisle seats.
applied.reasoningSummary: The active exchange is effectively closed: the latest user turn is a durable preference note, and the assistant confirmed it was stored. The earlier GC-debug/testing topic appears resolved enough to archive rather than keep raw. Durable preferences from t8 and t10 should survive as pins; the older troubleshooting/status turns are good eviction candidates. Because the conversation mostly closes after storing the preference, `resolve` fits better than `switch`.
